rationale: The citing paper’s main results include manipulations of the compromise effect (Studies 3–4) and it positions itself against prior context-effects work, while the cited paper is explicitly about alternative models of the compromise effect and local choice context. That direct construct overlap makes the citation plausibly foundational/theoretical rather than a generic mention. Even with only a single DOI-level matched reference in the raw evidence, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
